overview: This dashboard tracks trade policy interventions affecting strategic input commodities with direct or indirect exposure to the 2026 Iran-Hormuz crisis. All indicators count interventions announced since 28 February 2026, as recorded in the Global Trade Alert database. Data is refreshed daily.

products.name: Fuels
products.description: Direct exposure to crude supply disruptions, reduced refinery throughput, and constrained petroleum product exports through the Gulf/Hormuz corridor.

products.name: Fertilizers
products.description: Strong dependence on natural gas-derived ammonia and refinery-derived sulphur/sulphuric acid used in urea, ammonium sulphate, phosphates, DAP/MAP, and NPK production.

products.name: Sulphur
products.description: Sulphur is primarily recovered from oil refining and gas processing; reduced refinery operations directly constrain supply.

products.name: Methanol
products.description: Produced mainly from natural gas feedstocks; Middle East production/export infrastructure highly exposed to Gulf disruptions.

products.name: Graphite Feedstocks
products.description: Petroleum coke-based graphite feedstocks depend on refinery output and delayed coking capacity.

products.name: Alumina
products.description: Energy-intensive refining process indirectly exposed to higher fuel and gas prices caused by regional energy disruptions.

products.name: Helium
products.description: Significant global supply originates from Qatar and Gulf gas-processing infrastructure dependent on Hormuz shipping routes.

products.name: Monoethylene Glycol (MEG)
products.description: Petrochemical derivative produced from ethylene; exposed to disruptions in naphtha and gas-based cracker feedstocks.

products.name: Iron Ore
products.description: Primarily indirect exposure via higher bunker/freight costs and weaker steel-sector demand rather than refinery dependence.
